user_query:
You are my quality-control consultant. Build a local, pattern-based text scorecard and use it to evaluate two sample agent responses.

Read the two files under input/:
- input/baseline.txt — earlier response
- input/after.txt — revised response

Deliverables to create under output/:
1) output/scorecard_config.json — A self-contained JSON config that defines:
   - Five dimensions with rubrics, weights, and thresholds: accuracy, completeness, tone, format_compliance, consistency.
   - Aggregation method: weighted_average and overall pass threshold of 3.0.
   - Automated check settings consistent with the config below:
     • response_length: min_chars 100, max_chars 15000, ideal_min 300, ideal_max 8000, penalty_short 2, penalty_long 1
     • format_structure: expect_headers true; custom_patterns optional
     • sycophancy markers should include: "great question", "excellent question", "i'm so glad you asked"
     • filler_words with threshold_per_1000_chars 3 and a list including: "basically", "actually", "literally", "just"
     • hedge_words with threshold_per_1000_chars 4 and a list including: "might", "perhaps", "i think", "it seems"
     • required_sections: case-insensitive, require three sections: "Summary", "Recommendations", and "Risks"
     • style_consistency: max_sentence_length_std_dev 30
   - Ensure the DIMENSIONS are wired to these auto checks in a sensible way (e.g., tone uses sycophancy and filler_words; accuracy uses hedge_words; completeness uses required_sections and response_length; format_compliance uses format_structure; consistency uses style_consistency).

2) output/history.jsonl — Two JSON lines (one per evaluated file) with the following structure for each line:
   {
     "agent": "baseline" | "after",
     "task_type": "evaluation",
     "overall_score": <number between 1 and 5>,
     "pass": <true|false>,
     "dimensions": {
       "accuracy": {"score": <1-5>, "auto": {"hedge_per_1000": <number>}},
       "completeness": {"score": <1-5>, "auto": {"missing_sections": [..], "char_count": <int>}},
       "tone": {"score": <1-5>, "auto": {"sycophancy_hits": <int>, "filler_per_1000": <number>}},
       "format_compliance": {"score": <1-5>, "auto": {"has_headers": <true|false>}},
       "consistency": {"score": <1-5>, "auto": {"sentence_length_std": <number>}}
     }
   }
   Requirements:
   - Compute basic automated metrics using pattern matching on the text in input/ files.
   - Use per-1000-character rates for filler and hedge metrics.
   - The "baseline" entry must reflect at least one sycophancy hit and at least one missing required section; the "after" entry must have zero sycophancy hits and zero missing required sections.
   - The "after" overall_score must be at least 0.5 higher than the "baseline" overall_score and should be a pass (>= 3.0 aggregated by weighted_average).

3) output/compare.json — A summary comparing the two runs with per-dimension deltas and overall delta. Structure:
   {
     "baseline_overall": <number>,
     "after_overall": <number>,
     "overall_delta": <number>,
     "dimension_deltas": {
       "accuracy": <number>,
       "completeness": <number>,
       "tone": <number>,
       "format_compliance": <number>,
       "consistency": <number>
     }
   }
   Requirements:
   - Deltas are computed as after - baseline.
   - Deltas for tone, completeness, and format_compliance must be positive.
   - overall_delta must be >= 0.5.

4) output/report.json — A machine-readable single-evaluation summary of the "after" run with the following keys:
   {
     "agent": "after",
     "overall_score": <number>,
     "pass": <true|false>,
     "thresholds": {"overall": 3.0, "dimensions": {"accuracy": <number>, "completeness": <number>, "tone": <number>, "format_compliance": <number>, "consistency": <number>}},
     "notes": "..."
   }
   Include brief notes that reference which automated checks were triggered or cleared.

5) output/analysis.md — A plain-English narrative (at least 250 words) explaining:
   - What changed between baseline and after.
   - Specific references to sycophancy, filler words, required sections, and headers.
   - Why the after version scores higher according to the defined rubric.

Rules:
- Do not call any external services. Keep everything local and text-only.
- Use relative workspace paths exactly as specified above.
- Ensure numeric scores are between 1 and 5. Use consistent rounding.
- Be explicit in your calculations for per-1000-character rates and section detection (case-insensitive for required section headers).
